title: Add optimistic updates to task list mutations

commits.author: Mira Okafor
commits.subject: Add optimistic updates to task list

summary_md:
- Replaces the await-then-refetch pattern in `TaskList` with optimistic cache writes, so toggling or reordering a task feels instant instead of waiting ~300ms for the round-trip.
- Introduces a small `useOptimisticTasks` hook that wraps the mutation, snapshots the previous list, and rolls back on error.
- Extends the API client to accept an idempotency key per mutation and adds a toast when a rollback fires.

annotations.body_md: `onMutate` doesn't call `qc.cancelQueries(key)` first. If a background refetch lands between the optimistic write and the server response, it will clobber the optimistic state and the UI will flicker back to the old value.
Rollback restores the list but never surfaces the error. Consider wiring the existing `pushToast` here so users know the toggle didn't stick.

files.note_md: Adds a `variant="warning"` style and exports `pushToast`. Purely additive, no behaviour change for existing call sites.
Widens `Task.status` to include `"archived"` and adds an optional `updatedAt` timestamp. Type-only change.

suggestions: Add `await qc.cancelQueries(key)` at the top of `onMutate` in `useOptimisticTasks.ts`.
Move idempotency-key generation into the mutation context so retries reuse the same key.
Either consume `isPending` in `TaskRow` or remove it from the destructure to keep lint clean.
